scope_notes: Canonical projection registry for AI coding tools that csaw projects config into. v5 restructured to separate claim from evidence from freshness per cell, expanded to 9 kinds (added hooks, memory, ignore, settings), and scoped to 12 deeply-modeled tools. Other tools moved to watchlist. Designed with product-ambition shape (claim/evidence model, source provenance, drift-detection-ready) but lives inside csaw for now as docs/reference; intended to be liftable into a standalone registry product (configs.dev pattern) later.

design_intent.shape: Tool registry as structured data. Each cell is a normalized claim (where + what), tied to evidence (how we know), tied to freshness (when verified, by what method). Mirrors the models.dev pattern: tool/kind data lives in this file; generated outputs (docs, comparison matrices, audit reports) derive from it; future automation watches sources and proposes diffs against the claims.

phases.phase_1_now: Hand-curated registry with claim/evidence/freshness per cell. v5 = this state.
phases.phase_2_next: Snapshot script — `scripts/fetch-tool-sources.ts` fetches every source URL, normalizes, hashes, stores in `sources/snapshots/<tool>/`. No diffing yet; just baseline. Allows manual drift detection.
phases.phase_3_when_demand: Runtime verification fixtures — `fixtures/<tool>/` with test repos that prove discovery/parsing for top 5 tools. `make verify-tools` runs them non-destructively.
phases.phase_4_only_if_signal: Drift detection cron + LLM-proposed PRs (configs.dev pattern). Splits tools into per-file YAML, becomes a standalone product.

not_in_scope_yet: Per-tool YAML files (one JSON for now)

methodology.approach: Per tool: identify canonical docs, fetch ≥2 sources, triangulate against community lists (awesome-cli-coding-agents, dotagent's tool map, ECC adapter docs). Each cell records its evidence URLs explicitly.

confidence_tiers.runtime_verified: A fixture test proved the tool reads/parses this path. PHASE 3 — none yet.
confidence_tiers.source_verified: For OSS tools, source code inspection confirms the path. Not yet systematically applied.
confidence_tiers.verified: Official docs explicitly state this path/format.
confidence_tiers.documented: Official docs mention but with caveats; or doc is partial.
confidence_tiers.inferred: Assembled from secondary sources (community, blog, related docs).
confidence_tiers.unsupported: Tool does not have a documented file-based concept for this kind.

verification_methods.docs_match: Fetched the official doc and the claim matches what's stated.
verification_methods.schema_match: Found a schema reference or example JSON/YAML/TOML in the docs.
verification_methods.community_corroborated: Multiple non-official sources agree (DEV posts, GitHub repos using the path, awesome-* lists).
verification_methods.source_inspection: Read the tool's source code to confirm path lookup.
verification_methods.runtime_test: A fixture test demonstrated the tool discovers/parses the file.

known_limitations: Some tools (Trae, Lingma, Plandex) have docs primarily in non-English or behind login. Coverage may be incomplete.
Tools change rapidly. 'verified' cells today may drift in months.
Watchlist entries get shallow treatment; promote to deep when adoption signals warrant.
JetBrains-specific tools (Junie, IntelliJ AI Assistant) intentionally out of scope per user direction.
Cloud-only tools without local filesystem projection get minimal coverage by design.

version_history.v2: Expanded to ~25 tools. Caught some wrong claims (codex agents, cursor agents).
version_history.v3: Added Antigravity (Gemini CLI successor), Amazon Q, Factory, Qodo. Started flagging confidence.
version_history.v4: MAJOR corrections after user pushback: Hermes IS cross-tool reader (reads .cursorrules, etc.). Goose IS file-projectable (.goosehints + .goose/recipes/). OpenHands has .openhands/microagents/. Cody has .vscode/cody.json. Added universal_paths section + mcp_comparison matrix.
version_history.v5: Restructured to claim/evidence/freshness model. Added 4 new kinds (hooks, memory, ignore, settings). Scoped MVP to 12 tools; others moved to watchlist. Documented phase plan for future evolution toward configs.dev-style registry product.

kinds_definitions.instructions: Always-on project guidance. AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, .hermes.md.
kinds_definitions.rules: Always-on coding/style rules. Cursor rules, Windsurf rules, Copilot instructions, Continue rules.
kinds_definitions.skills: On-demand procedural workflows. SKILL.md folders, slash commands, prompts, recipes, workflows.
kinds_definitions.mcp: Model Context Protocol server configs. Per-tool schema and scope vary widely.
kinds_definitions.hooks: Lifecycle scripts triggered by tool events (file save, agent stop, pre/post tool use). Claude hooks, Kiro hooks, Copilot CLI hooks.
kinds_definitions.memory: Persistent context across sessions. Claude memory/agent-memory, Goose hints, Hermes memories.
kinds_definitions.ignore: Context exclusion patterns. .cody/ignore, .cursorignore, .aiderignore, .tongyiignore. Like .gitignore for AI tools.
kinds_definitions.settings: General tool config that may contain multiple kinds. ~/.claude/settings.json, opencode.json, ~/.config/goose/config.yaml.

csaw_relevance_legend.recommended-add: Clean file-based projection that fits csaw's current model.
csaw_relevance_legend.consider: File-based projection but with structural mismatches (suffixes, single-file targets, git-visibility).
csaw_relevance_legend.watch: On watchlist; emerging or limited filesystem story.
csaw_relevance_legend.deprecated: Tool being sunset; keep entry for history.

universal_paths.description: Paths read by MULTIPLE tools. Projecting these gives csaw cross-tool leverage. Listed in order of breadth.

AGENTS.md.description: De facto cross-tool instructions standard (AAIF / Linux Foundation). 60K+ repos.

CLAUDE.md.description: Claude Code's instructions. Multi-tool fallback for Claude-compatibility.

.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md.description: agentskills.io open standard. 1000+ skills in ecosystem.

.claude/agents/.description: Claude Code's agent definitions. Cross-read by VS Code Copilot.

.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md.description: Antigravity's primary skill path. csaw's StandardFallback.
.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md.csaw_status: Already StandardFallback. csaw serves Antigravity through this.

.cursor/rules/*.mdc.csaw_status: Already projected via cursor tool entry.

.cursorrules.description: Cursor's legacy single-file rules. Hermes also reads.

~/.cursor/mcp.json.description: Cursor's user-scope MCP. Trae reads as global fallback.

~/.copilot/agents/.description: User-scope Copilot agents — SHARED by VS Code Copilot AND Copilot CLI.
~/.copilot/agents/.csaw_status: Convergence point. Treat as one tool when Copilot epic happens.

claude.scope_notes: Anthropic's official CLI. Originator of .claude/agents/ and SKILL.md folder patterns — now agentskills.io open standard.

project.file_pattern: single file (walked up to repo root)

rules.notes: No separate rules concept. Path-specific rules live in CLAUDE.md.

agents.notes: Identity from `name` not path. Precedence: enterprise > user > project > plugin.

mcp.notes: OAuth via /mcp command. MCP_TIMEOUT env var sets startup timeout.

hooks.notes: Per-skill hooks via SKILL.md frontmatter; per-agent hooks via agent frontmatter; settings-level hooks in settings.json.

memory.notes: Per-subagent persistent memory. user/project/local scopes. -local variant is gitignored.

ignore.notes: Claude Code respects .gitignore for file enumeration. No Claude-specific ignore file.

mcp.notes: MCP ONLY in config.toml. Closest .codex/config.toml wins.

hooks.notes: Also configurable via [hooks] table in config.toml.

ignore.notes: Respects .gitignore. project_doc_max_bytes / project_doc_fallback_filenames in config.toml control AGENTS.md discovery.

settings.notes: Single config.toml contains agents, MCP, hooks, all settings.

cursor.scope_notes: Cursor CLI (Jan 2026) shares config with desktop.

rules.notes: User rules in app settings (not filesystem). .cursorrules legacy but still read; Hermes also reads it.

agents.notes: Cursor 'Background Agents' are runtime cloud sessions.

mcp.notes: Trae also reads ~/.cursor/mcp.json as user-global fallback.

hooks.notes: Configured in IDE settings, not file-based.

memory.notes: Cursor has chat history but no documented persistent memory file.

settings.notes: Settings in Cursor app (not filesystem). Workspace settings can ride along in .vscode/settings.json.

opencode.scope_notes: OSS leader (150K+ stars). EXPLICIT cross-tool compat — reads .claude/ and .agents/ as skill fallbacks.

agents.notes: Filename = agent name. mode: subagent | primary.

skills.notes: SIX paths. csaw projects .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ → free OpenCode coverage.

mcp.notes: Tokens stored at ~/.local/share/opencode/mcp-auth.json. Per-agent tool overrides supported.

hooks.notes: Plugin events / hook adapter mentioned in ECC cross-harness doc; OpenCode native hook story unclear.

settings.notes: Single config holds MCP, agent config, model preferences.

env_var_substitution: variable interpolation in command, args, env, serverUrl, url, headers

mcp.notes: User-scope ONLY. 100 total tools limit across all MCP servers.

memory.notes: Cascade memories — manage via 'Manage memories' UI.

vscode-copilot.scope_notes: Mandatory filename suffixes. User-scope agents at ~/.copilot/agents/ SHARED with Copilot CLI.

agents.notes: Reads .claude/agents/ for compat. SHARES ~/.copilot/agents/ with Copilot CLI. .chatmode.md DEPRECATED.

schema.sandboxing: macOS/Linux stdio: sandboxEnabled + sandbox object for fs/network

mcp.notes: VS Code's NATIVE MCP. Top-level `servers` NOT `mcpServers`.

hooks.notes: Via VS Code extension API, not file-based.

settings.notes: VS Code workspace settings (Copilot-related keys nested).

copilot-cli.scope_notes: Shares ~/.copilot/ with VS Code Copilot — converging Copilot config surface.

antigravity.scope_notes: Gemini CLI's successor. Built in Go. Uses .agents/ (plural) — same as csaw's StandardFallback. csaw already serves skills.

agents.notes: Single file enumerating agents, not folder-per-agent.

project.file_pattern: folder containing SKILL.md AND/OR skills.md declaration

skills.notes: Same SKILL.md folder as Claude (agentskills.io). csaw's .agents/skills/ fallback SERVES Antigravity for free.

schema.top_level_key: (MCP inside settings; exact key inferred from Gemini CLI heritage)

hooks.notes: Hooks supported (inherited from Gemini CLI); paths unclear.

memory.notes: Antigravity 2.0 uses ~/.gemini/antigravity/ for brain entries, scratch space, conversation files.

kiro.scope_notes: AWS agentic coding IDE on Bedrock. Spec-driven.

skills.notes: Kiro 'specs' (Requirements/Design/Tasks in EARS notation) — not Claude-style skills semantically.

mcp.notes: MCP supported but exact paths not in fetched docs.

hooks.notes: Kiro hooks trigger on file events (created/saved/deleted), prompt/agent lifecycle, spec task events, manual.

continue.scope_notes: Has .continue/rules/ (project) and ~/.continue/rules/ (user) — file-based, NOT all nested in config.

rules.notes: Hub-based rules referenced via `uses:` in config.yaml. Name with 01-, 02- for order.

mcp.notes: Nested in config — not file-projectable separately.

cline.scope_notes: Formerly Claude Dev. VS Code + JetBrains + CLI. Roo Code is popular fork.

instructions.notes: .clinerules can be file or directory. Workspace > global.

agents.notes: No file-based subagents (Cline has modes but runtime-only).

skills.notes: Skills feature mentioned but file convention unclear.

mcp.notes: MCP managed in Cline IDE settings panel.

goose.scope_notes: MOVED from block/goose to aaif-goose/goose. .goosehints + .goose/recipes/ are real file-based concepts (corrected in v4 from prior under-documentation).

instructions.notes: Project + user scope. Subdirectory walk combines hints up to repo root. Respects .gitignore.

agents.notes: Discovery order: current dir → GOOSE_RECIPE_PATH env → ~/.config/goose/recipes/ → .goose/recipes/ → GitHub via GOOSE_RECIPE_GITHUB_REPO env.

skills.notes: Recipes serve as both agents and skills (workflows).

mcp.notes: Goose extensions = MCP servers. 70+ extensions. Configured via `goose configure` CLI.

watchlist.description: Tools with file-based projection that are less prioritized for csaw's MVP scope. Promoted to deep entries when adoption signals warrant. Each watchlist entry includes the key project/user paths but with less detail than deep entries.

gemini.scope_notes: DEPRECATED — sunset 2026-06-18. Migration target: Antigravity. Gemini Code Assist Standard/Enterprise license holders retain access.

roo-code.scope_notes: Cline fork. Strongest file-based mode system.

kilo-code.scope_notes: Reads .claude/ and .agents/ — csaw's projection serves Kilo for free.

zed.scope_notes: Reads .rules at project root. MCP via settings.json context_servers (user-scope).

hermes.scope_notes: CROSS-TOOL READER. Reads .hermes.md/HERMES.md (own), AGENTS.md (universal), CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, .cursor/rules/*.mdc. csaw projecting .cursor/rules/ serves Hermes for free.

claw-code.scope_notes: Clean-room rewrite of Claude Code. Reuses .claude/ — csaw projection serves automatically.

factory.scope_notes: AGENTS.md only — already served by csaw's instructions.

qodo.scope_notes: Code review tool, different model. Hierarchical best_practices.md.

plandex.scope_notes: Plan-first CLI. Dotfile conventions less standardized.

mcp_comparison.description: Side-by-side MCP comparison. Deep tools first, watchlist tools at end. Tools missing here nest MCP in multi-purpose config files or don't support file-based MCP.

opencode.auth: OAuth auto (RFC 7591 dynamic registration), static OAuth, API key headers
opencode.extras: Tool filtering with glob patterns. Tokens at ~/.local/share/opencode/mcp-auth.json

continue.extras: Nested in config.yaml — not file-projectable separately

goose.key: extensions (NOT mcpServers — nested in goose config.yaml)

gemini.extras: Most field-rich schema: includeTools, excludeTools, trust, targetAudience, targetServiceAccount, authProviderType

agents_md_universal_standard.description: AGENTS.md is the cross-tool standard, Linux Foundation stewarded. 60K+ repos. ~32 tools researched read it.
agents_md_universal_standard.csaw_impact: csaw's instructions kind serves ~32 tools via universal AGENTS.md.

agentskills_open_standard.description: Anthropic's SKILL.md folder format = agentskills.io open standard. Adopted by Claude, Cursor, Gemini, Codex, Antigravity, OpenCode, Kilo Code, Hermes (compatible), OpenClaw. 1000+ skills.
agentskills_open_standard.csaw_impact: csaw's skill projection (.claude/skills/, .agents/skills/) serves agentskills.io ecosystem automatically.

dot_agents_directory_convention.description: Newer tools converge on .<toolname>/{agents,rules,skills,commands,mcp-configs}/: Kiro, CodeBuddy, Antigravity (.agents/), Roo Code, JoyCode, Qwen, Kilo Code, Amazon Q (.amazonq/rules/), Cline (.clinerules/), Augment (.augment/rules/), Continue (.continue/rules/), OpenHands (.openhands/microagents/), Goose (.goose/recipes/).
dot_agents_directory_convention.csaw_impact: csaw's ToolDir struct fits this pattern.

mcp_schema_divergence.description: 6 distinct top-level keys: mcpServers (most), servers (VS Code), mcp (OpenCode), mcp_servers (Codex TOML), context_servers (Zed), extensions (Goose).
mcp_schema_divergence.csaw_impact: Per-tool registry files projected to per-tool paths is the right model.

no_user_scope_filesystem.description: Cursor user rules, VS Code Copilot user instructions, Continue IDE settings, Augment user guidelines, Devin playbooks, Zed user agents live in app settings / cloud UIs.
no_user_scope_filesystem.csaw_impact: Out of scope for csaw's filesystem projection.

github_copilot_convergence.description: VS Code Copilot + Copilot CLI share ~/.copilot/agents/.
github_copilot_convergence.csaw_impact: Future Copilot epic should be ONE `copilot` tool entry.

chatmodes_deprecated_for_agents.description: VS Code Copilot's .chatmode.md DEPRECATED. Rename to .agent.md.

filename_suffix_requirement.description: GitHub Copilot (both surfaces) requires mandatory suffixes: .agent.md, .instructions.md, .prompt.md.
filename_suffix_requirement.csaw_impact: Supporting Copilot requires per-subdir suffix support in ToolDir.

github_dir_git_tracked_default.csaw_impact: Supporting .github/ tools requires per-tool CommitToGit flag.

trae_cursor_mcp_sharing.csaw_impact: csaw's projection to .cursor/mcp.json serves Trae at user level.

antigravity_replaces_gemini_cli.description: Google sunset Gemini CLI 2026-06-18. Antigravity CLI uses .agents/ (csaw's StandardFallback).

agentic_ai_foundation_governance.description: AGENTS.md, MCP, Goose all governed by AAIF under Linux Foundation.
agentic_ai_foundation_governance.csaw_impact: Bet on these standards being durable.

hermes_as_cross_tool_reader.description: Hermes reads .cursorrules, .cursor/rules/*.mdc, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, plus its own .hermes.md.
hermes_as_cross_tool_reader.csaw_impact: csaw projecting tool-X paths can serve cross-tool readers like Hermes for free.

openhands_microagents_v0_v1_naming.description: OpenHands V0 calls them 'microagents', V1 calls them 'skills'. Same .openhands/microagents/ path.
openhands_microagents_v0_v1_naming.csaw_impact: Map csaw's agents OR skills kind to OpenHands' microagents directory.

mcp_is_standard_but_config_is_not.description: MCP itself is an open standard. MCP CLIENT config is per-tool. 6+ schemas, 5+ transports, varied auth.
mcp_is_standard_but_config_is_not.csaw_impact: csaw's strongest product wedge may be the translation/compatibility map across MCP client configs — not just file projection.

competitive_landscape.description: Adjacent projects in csaw's space. csaw differentiates by governance (priority, protection, audit, fork, lineage).

dotagent.description: Multi-file AI agent configuration manager. Single source of truth across ~15 tools.
dotagent.vs_csaw: Narrower scope — file-level multi-tool mapping. No governance.

dot-agents.vs_csaw: Global-first centralization vs csaw's project-aware multi-source composition.

ECC.description: 182K stars. Adapter architecture for ~14 tools. ECC Pro $19/seat/mo.
ECC.vs_csaw: Adapter-per-tool + curated content distribution. csaw is mount-not-install for user-curated sources.

models.dev.description: Open-source database of AI models. Per-provider TOML files, public API.
models.dev.vs_csaw: Different domain (models, not tool configs) but the SHAPE this projection registry should aspire to.

openclaw.description: Multi-channel personal AI assistant with derivative ecosystem.

in_code_now.status: Instructions + skills. Agents/MCP TOML inline — intentional out of scope.
Rules + MCP + instructions. Skills (.cursor/commands/) NOT projected.
Agents + skills + instructions. MCP nested — intentional out of scope.
Agents + instructions. DEPRECATED 2026-06-18 (now in watchlist as deprecated).

recommended_additions_phase_1.reason: Gemini CLI's successor. csaw's .agents/skills/ ALREADY serves it.

recommended_additions_phase_2.reason: AGENTS.md cascading — already served via instructions.

needs_structural_work_before_adding.needs: Per-subdir filename suffix (.agent.md, .instructions.md, .prompt.md). Single-file instruction target. Per-tool CommitToGit flag.
Project .vscode/cody.json is JSON-with-custom-commands. csaw can project the file but content schema is Cody-specific.
csaw already serves via .cursor/rules/ + AGENTS.md. Could add explicit .hermes.md/HERMES.md target.
Only project paths are AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md (already served). MCP in ~/.pi/agent/ — out of scope.

out_of_scope_with_current_model.reason: Settings.json user-scope only. Project .rules is single file.
AGENTS.md only — already served by csaw's instructions.

additional_tools_known_to_exist_not_rese.description: Tools from various catalogues not deep-researched. Most lack documented or significant filesystem config.
additional_tools_known_to_exist_not_rese.rationale: Filtered out: small user base, no documented filesystem convention, reference/educational, cloud-only, or out of scope per user direction.
